# Extended Case References — Lecture ZWgkUrtyK-4

*Brief mentions of canonical cases that did not warrant a full `case_index.md` entry. Generated by reconciliation pass against the 1,676-cluster canon. Substantive treatments are in `case_index.md`.*

## Cases mentioned in passing

### 1980s Delrin toilet failures
- **canonical_cluster_id:** "1980s Delrin toilet failures"
- **Anchor:** `§8.p9`
- **Quote (layer 3):** "What do they make out of Delrin? They had the little water closet valves. You flush the toilet and it fills up with the float and then it shuts off. They were making those things out of Delrin. After about two or three years, the chlorine in the water would cause the Delrin to turn into a brittle plastic, full of little cracks. They had what they called the exploding toilets. When this thing would let go, the sixty psi water pressure would hit the little ceramic top of your water closet, and it would blow off. It wasn't really an explosion. It wasn't a release of gas pressure. It was water..."
